page: Amazon Web Services m8azn.xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized server instance running on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 9R05 processor with 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs. The processor operates at a high clock speed of 5.0 GHz and includes 20 MB of L3 cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 48 KB of L1d cache. System memory consists of 16.0 GB of DDR5 RAM operating at 6 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or graphics processing units.

Benchmark evaluations reveal top-tier single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core performance, Geekbench single-core scores, and single-threaded gzip and bzip3 operations all ranking in the top 10%. Multi-core metrics, such as stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, fall within the average range. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance, with uncached large-block reads at 103.7 GB/sec and writes at 68.41 GB/sec. For language model tasks, the instance achieves top-tier text generation speeds of 452.13 tokens/sec for small models, while medium model inference remains average.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m8azn.xlarge offers high-density single-thread compute performance relative to its resource class, though the lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on external EBS volumes for persistent storage. Network performance is constrained to an 8 Gbps baseline. This hardware profile is highly suited for workloads requiring strong single-threaded execution, such as text processing, web serving, lightweight software compilation, database operations, and small-scale language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m8azn.xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9R05 processor running at 5.0 GHz, paired with 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 6 GHz. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on an 8 Gbps baseline network speed. Benchmark results show top-tier single-core performance, including stress-ng single-core and single-threaded compression tests, while multi-core metrics and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier. This resource profile delivers strong qualitative cost efficiency for single-thread intensive tasks. It is well-suited for web serving, database operations, software compilation, and small-scale LLM inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8azn.xlarge is a general-purpose Nitro instance featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC cores at 5.0 GHz, 16 GB of DDR5 RAM, and top-tier single-core benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8azn.xlarge is a general-purpose Nitro instance with 4 dedicated AMD EPYC cores at 5.0 GHz and 16 GB of DDR5 RAM.
tagline: This high frequency virtual server delivers top tier single core performance and fast DDR5 memory for compute intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated 4-core AMD EPYC 9R05 processor running at 5.0 GHz
16.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 6 GHz
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor optimization
Best suited for web serving, database operations, and lightweight LLM inference
